title: Progress Is Governable
dek: Income draws a line. Health systems decide the distance from it.

beats.label: Beat 1
beats.headline: Income sets the baseline, not the outcome
beats.body: The first view teaches the shared rule: wealth predicts a lot, but countries still spread above and below expectation. The distance to the line is the story object.
beats.aria_summary: Global scatter and expectation line showing typical and tail gaps in life expectancy.

beats.label: Beat 2
beats.headline: Rwanda bends the trajectory
beats.body: The reveal is time-based first, model-based second: under-5 mortality drops from 98.3 to 42.0, while Rwanda's position shifts from near/below expectation to above it.
beats.aria_summary: Rwanda trajectory with before-and-after under-5 card and movement against the same expectation line.

beats.label: Beat 3
beats.headline: A second country confirms the pattern
beats.body: Vietnam is not a one-off blip. In 2010 it sits about 11 years above its income-based life-expectancy expectation, confirming the same directional signal on a different path.
beats.aria_summary: Vietnam and Rwanda highlighted above the same expectation line to confirm pattern beyond one country.
